People/John-K-Palmer.title: John K Palmer
People/John-K-Palmer.content:
John K Palmer
Biographical Profile

Name: John K Palmer
Role in this project: Palmer-line patriarch spanning Wisconsin (Sauk and Eau Claire counties) with documented household progression from 1860-1900.

Source-Cited Facts

Birth/Death: Born 9 Oct 1821; died 2 Jun 1906 (age 84 years, 7 months, 24 days).
Birthplace: Pennsylvania
Occupation: Farmer

Census Records and Household Context
1860 Wisconsin Census — Sauk County, Baraboo

Head: J PALMER, male, age 38, occupation farmer, property $400, born Pennsylvania
Wife: Jane PALMER, female, age 36, born Pennsylvania
Children:

Mary PALMER, female, age 15, born Pennsylvania
Elizabeth PALMER, female, age 14, born Pennsylvania
W H PALMER, male, age 9, born Pennsylvania (later William Henry Palmer)
Emily PALMER, female, age 7, born Pennsylvania


Source: Series M653, Roll 1429, Page 450; GSU microfilm available

1880 Wisconsin Census — Eau Claire County, Otter Creek Township, Page 532A

Head: John K. PALMER, male, self, married, age 48, born Pennsylvania, occupation farmer
Wife: Jane PALMER, female, married, age 55, born Pennsylvania, occupation keeping house
Children:

Rosella PALMER, female, single, age 16, born Wisconsin
Peter PALMER, male, single, age 14, born Wisconsin


Source: Fam Hist Lib Film 1255425; GSU microfilm available

1900 Wisconsin Census — Eau Claire County, Augusta, Perkins Street, Sheet 4B

Head: John K. PALMER, male, race White, birthdate Oct 1821, age 78, born Pennsylvania, occupation day laborer
Wife: Jane PALMER, female, race White, birthdate Aug 1824, age 75, born Pennsylvania
Note: Occupation changed to day laborer by 1900; both in advanced age
Source: Series T623, Roll 1787, Page 4B; GSU microfilm available

Family Connections

Wife: Jane Palmer (b. Aug 1824 Pennsylvania, age 75 in 1900)
Children identified: Mary (b. ~1845), Elizabeth (b. ~1846), William H. (b. ~1851), Emily (b. ~1853), Rosella (b. ~1864), Peter (b. ~1866)
Son: William Henry Palmer (1851-1927), who continued farming in Minnesota
Pedigree significance: Patriarch of Wisconsin Palmer branch; father of William Henry who migrated to Minnesota and whose daughter May Aleen married into the Prior line

Family Diagram

graph TD
  JKP["John K. Palmer<br/>1821-1906<br/>Farmer → Day Laborer"]
  JP["Jane Palmer<br/>1824-?<br/>Pennsylvania"]
  
  WHP["William H.<br/>1851-1927<br/>→ Minnesota/Iowa"]
  
  JKP --- JP
  JKP --> WHP

John K. Palmer was the patriarch of the Wisconsin Palmer line (1821-1906), father of William Henry Palmer whose line extended into Minnesota and Iowa.
Research Gaps


                  
                  Priority Research Leads 
                  
                

The following census records are indicated in the pedigree diagrams but matching transcripts are missing from the vault:

1890 Census: Transcript needed to verify household context. (Note: The 1890 US Federal Census was largely destroyed by fire)

People/John-McIntyre-Lemmon.title: John McIntyre Lemmon
People/John-McIntyre-Lemmon.content:
John McIntyre Lemmon
Biographical Profile

Name: John McIntyre Lemmon
Role in this project: Lemmon-line son of Uriah Blake Lemmon and Emily Amanda MacIntyre, documented in census summaries and a Sandusky/Ottawa County biographical sketch.

Source-Cited Facts

The Sandusky-Beers biographical sketch states that John McIntyre Lemmon was born 25 July 1839 in Townsend, Sandusky County, Ohio.
The same sketch identifies his parents as Uriah Blake Lemmon and Emily Amanda MacIntyre Lemmon.
It identifies his paternal grandfather as James Lemmon, who married Rebecca Blake in 1805.
The sketch says John McIntyre Lemmon studied law, enlisted during the Civil War, served in Ohio volunteer infantry units, and was promoted from private to second lieutenant on 23 May 1862 and captain on 23 July 1863.
It states he married Annie Covell on 29 March 1864 at Perkins, Erie County, Ohio.
After returning from war service, the sketch says he settled in Clyde, Ohio, practiced law, was elected the first mayor of Clyde on 11 June 1866, and was appointed judge of the common pleas court in October 1866.
It states he died at his home in Clyde on 17 August 1895.

Family Connections

Father: Uriah Blake Lemmon
Mother: Emily Amanda MacIntyre
Paternal grandparents: James Lemmon and Rebecca Blake
Spouse: Annie Covell
Known census connection: Appears as John M. Lemmon in the 1850 Uriah Blake Lemmon household and as the 1870 Clyde household head where Uriah Blake Lemmon lived with him.

Research Gaps

Verify military unit details from service or pension records.
Confirm marriage to Annie Covell from county marriage records.
Confirm death date from cemetery, probate, obituary, or civil death records.
Determine whether descendants or collateral records clarify the Lemmon-Thorpe naming connection.

Sources

Book Outprints — Sandusky-Beers Lemmon John M
Shared Intake 2026-04-22 Census Summary Individuals p31-p40

People/John-McIntyre-of-Kilmoore.content:
John McIntyre of Kilmoore
Biographical Profile

Name: John McIntyre
Disambiguation: Of Kilmoore, County Tyrone, Ireland.
Role in this project: Older compiled McIntyre-line ancestor named in the MacIntyre book outprint.

Source-Cited Facts

The MacIntyre book outprint identifies John McIntyre as born in the parish of Kilmoore, County Tyrone, Ireland, about 1678.
The same source says he came from Ireland before 1719.
The source gives his death as 26 May 1759 at Rutland, Massachusetts.
The source says he married Grace, who survived him.
Pages 161-162 list children of John and Grace McIntyre, including Agnes, Janet, Elizabeth, Martha, Mary, Christian, John, James, and Thomas.

Research Gaps

Verify the County Tyrone origin and pre-1719 migration with primary or near-contemporary records.
Confirm the child list in Massachusetts town, church, probate, or land records.
Determine whether the line from John McIntyre of Kilmoore to William McIntyre is fully documented or partly inferred in the compiled source.

Sources

Book Outprints — MacIntyre

People/John-Miller.title: John Miller
People/John-Miller.content:
John Miller
Biographical Profile

Name: John Miller
Role in this project: Compiled Miller-line ancestor and father of Mathias Miller in the Samuel Miller book outprint.

Source-Cited Facts

The Genealogy and History of Samuel Miller outprint lists John Miller as a child of Samuel Miller.
Page 15 says John Miller was born in York County, Pennsylvania, and moved with his parents to Rockingham County, Virginia, about 1782.
Page 15 says John Miller married Anna Smootz.
Page 15 says John Miller moved to Fairfield County, Ohio, in 1805 and settled about two miles south of Millersport, Ohio, on the farm later owned by Lewis Hite.
Page 240 says John Miller married Anna Smootz and had four known children: Mathias Miller, Anna Miller, Jacob Miller, and Polly Miller.

Family Connections

Father: Samuel Miller, based on the compiled Miller genealogy.
Spouse: Anna Smootz.
Child: Mathias Miller.

The Thorpe pedigree timeline places John Tallman between Benjamin Tallman and Benjamin B Tallman in the Tallman chain.
Settlers by the Long Grey Trail page 375 says litigation over Nathaniel Harrison’s heirs identified Elizabeth Harrison as wife of John Tallman.
Page 375 says a 10 March 1817 deed included John Tallman and wife Elizabeth, of Fairfield County, Ohio, conveying their share of Nathaniel Harrison land.
Page 376 says Elizabeth Harrison married John Tallman in 1808 and removed to Ohio.

People/Lenore-Hetty-Tallman.title: Lenore Hetty Tallman
People/Lenore-Hetty-Tallman.content:
Lenore Hetty Tallman
Biographical Profile

Name: Lenore Hetty Tallman
Role in this project: Tallman-line descendant appearing as daughter in late-19th and early-20th century census-summary entries.

Source-Cited Facts

A census-summary entry gives Lenore Hetty Tallman as born 1 Feb 1879 and died 28 Jun 1953.
The 1880 Afton Township, Cherokee County, Iowa extract lists Lena Tallman as daughter, age 1.
The 1900 Sioux City, Woodbury County, Iowa extract lists Lena H. Tallman as daughter, born Feb 1879, age 21, single.
The Burial Sites book places Lenore Hetty Tallman at Evergreen Cemetery in Grand Mound, Iowa (page 37), with date of death 28 June 1953. Map: Google Maps.
The Genealogy and History of Samuel Miller page 242 lists Elvin Lenora Tallman, born 7 February 1879, as a child of Mathias M. Tallman and Lizzie Wimmer; the same page separately summarizes this child as Lenore Tallman.
Page 250 of the same source says Elvin Lenora Tallman married U. B. Thorpe on 22 October 1903 and had a child Ralph Thorpe born 27 October 1906.

Research Gaps

Confirm whether Lena/Lena H./Lenore Hetty in compiled entries all refer to the same person.
Extend record coverage beyond 1900 and reconcile later-name variants.
Confirm parent-child links from primary census images.
Reconcile the exact birth date discrepancy: census/burial summaries give 1 February 1879, while the Miller genealogy gives 7 February 1879 for Elvin Lenora Tallman.

People/Lester-Harold-Spicer.title: Lester Harold Spicer
People/Lester-Harold-Spicer.content:
Lester Harold Spicer
Biographical Profile

Name: Lester Harold Spicer
Birth/Death: Born 14 Jul 1906; died 28 Jun 1974 (age 67 years, 11 months, 14 days)
Role in this project: Third-generation Spicer; son of George B. Spicer (farmer); transition from agricultural to industrial occupation (car repairer); married Ruby Bernice Prior.
Burial: Cedar Memorial Cemetery, Cedar Rapids, Iowa; 64K Last Supper, Space 4; GPS 42°1’24.2”N 91°38’17.4”W; inscription LESTER H. / 1906 † 1974; Burial Sites book, page 33.

Census Records and Life Progression
1910 Iowa Census — Benton County, Canton Township (as young child)

Head: George Spicer, male, age 45, farmer
Mother: Hattie Spicer, female, age 32
Lester H. Spicer appears as son, male, age 3
Siblings: Edith (9), Charles R. (8), George G. (6), Chester J. (2.3)
Uncle: William Spicer, male, age 49 (farmer laborer)
Source: Series T624, Roll 391, Pages 56-57

1920 Iowa Census — Benton County, Benton Township (as adolescent)

Head: George Spicer, male, age 55, farmer
Mother: Hattie Spicer, female, age 42
Lester H. Spicer appears as son, male, age 13
Siblings: Charles R. (17, farm laborer), George J. (15, farm laborer), Chester J. (11), Edna (7), Myron (4+)
Note: Older brothers already working as farm laborers; Lester still in school/home
Source: Series T625, Roll 477, Pages 4B & 5A, ED 4

1930 Iowa Census — Linn County, Clinton Township (as young adult)

Head: George Spicer, male, age 65, farmer
Mother: Hattie Spicer, female, age 53
Lester H. Spicer, male, age 23, car repairer (railroad shop) — occupational shift away from agriculture
Siblings: Chester J. (21, farmer), Edna (17), Myron L. (14)
Source: Series T626, Roll 664, Page 11A, ED 8

1940 Iowa Census — Linn County, Cedar Rapids, 17th Ward (as adult)

Head: Lester H. Spicer, male, age 32, torch operator, rock crushing manufacturer
Wife: Ruby B. Spicer, female, age 26
Son: Richard Spicer, male, age 4
Address: 724 Seventh Street SW, Cedar Rapids
Note: Established in town with manufacturing job; one child; urban rather than rural residence
Source: Series T9, Roll [unlisted], Page [unlisted], ED [unlisted]

Family Connections and Life Trajectory


Father: George B. Spicer (1864-1938) — patriarch farmer


Mother: Hattie May Risden (1877-1967) — daughter of Sarah Annett Lemmon/Monroe Thorpe


Wife: Ruby Bernice Prior — married by 1940


Son: Richard Spicer (age 4 in 1940)


Siblings: Edith, Charles Russell, George G., Chester J., Edna, Myron L.


Occupational Transition
Lester Harold Spicer represents the agricultural-to-industrial transition of the 1920s-1940s:

1910-1920: Child of farmer, in agricultural household
1920: Age 13, farming household (brothers working as farm laborers)
1930: Age 23, occupational pivot — car repairer for railroad (skilled industrial trade, not farm labor)
1940: Age 32, torch operator in rock crushing factory; established in Cedar Rapids (urban center)

Broader Spicer Line Context
Four-generation progression:

Nathan Spicer (1796-1873): Patriarch, blacksmith/farm laborer origin
Charles Russell Spicer (1822-1887): Son, farmer/chair maker transition
George B. Spicer (1864-1938): Grandson, prosperous farmer with 7+ children
Lester Harold Spicer (1906-1974): Great-grandson, industrial worker; leaves agriculture

Research Gaps

Confirm marriage date to Ruby Bernice Prior
Clarify Richard Spicer’s full parentage (1940 census shows him at age 4)
Verify torch operator/rock crushing manufacturer position (likely Central Iowa cement/gravel operations)
Confirm death date and location from vital records

People/Louis-S-Jr-Bangle.title: Louis S Jr Bangle
People/Louis-S-Jr-Bangle.content:
Louis S Jr Bangle
Biographical Profile

Name: Louis S Jr Bangle
Role in this project: Bangle-line ancestor with repeated household appearances in 1860-1880 US census-summary entries.

Source-Cited Facts

A census-summary entry gives Louis S Jr Bangle as born 1 Dec 1823 and died 5 Feb 1893.
The 1860 Bridge Creek Township, Eau Claire County, Michigan entry lists Lewis Bangle as a farmer.
The 1870 Otter Creek Township, Eau Claire County, Wisconsin entry lists L. S. BANGLE, age 46, farmer.
The 1880 Otter Creek Township entry lists Lewis S. BANGLE, age 56, with wife Angeline and children including Welthy O., William H., and Robert E.
The Burial Sites book places Louis S. Bangle at Augusta, Wisconsin (page 5), with date of death 5 Feb 1893 and inscription L.S. BANGLE / BORN DEC 1, 1822 / DIED FEB. 5, 1893 / AGED 69 YRS 1 MO. Map: Google Maps.

Research Gaps

Confirm exact birth/death evidence for Louis S Jr Bangle beyond compiled summary dates.
Reconcile spelling variants Lewis/Louis across extracted tables.
Validate household composition from image-level census pages.

People/Major-McIntyre.title: Major McIntyre
People/Major-McIntyre.content:
Major McIntyre
Biographical Profile

Name: Major McIntyre
Role in this project: McIntyre-line burial-book entry associated with Sarah Shutts McIntyre.

Source-Cited Facts

The Burial Sites book places Major McIntyre at Tew Cemetery in Townsend Township, Sandusky County, Ohio (page 19), Section 25-17-5, Lat. 412203N Long. 0825125W.
The page gives a date of death of 28 July 1842 and inscription Major Mcintyre / Died July 28 1842 / Æ 53 ys 2 m & 26d.
The same book comments that Sarah Shutts McIntyre’s stone, on the next page, is right next to Major McIntyre’s stone and may have been carved by the same stone carver.
Map: Google Maps.
The MacIntyre book outprint lists Major as a child of William McIntyre and Rosanna Campbell McIntyre, with birth date 3 May 1789.
The 3 May 1789 birth date is consistent with the Burial Sites inscription age of 53 years, 2 months, and 26 days at death on 28 July 1842.

Family Connections

Father: William McIntyre, based on the compiled MacIntyre book outprint.
Mother: Rosanna Campbell, based on the compiled MacIntyre book outprint.
Possible spouse or close relative: Sarah Shutts, based only on burial proximity and the Burial Sites note; the relationship still needs non-burial confirmation.

Research Gaps


                  
                  Priority Research Leads 
                  
                

The following census records are indicated in the pedigree diagrams but matching transcripts are missing from the vault:

1840 Census: Transcript needed to verify household context.

People/Martha-Eliza-Lewis.title: Martha Eliza Lewis
People/Martha-Eliza-Lewis.content:
Martha Eliza Lewis
Biographical Profile

Name: Martha Eliza Lewis (later Martha Eliza Prior)
Role in this project: Lewis-to-Prior branch matriarch spanning Wisconsin (1860 childhood) to Minnesota/South Dakota (1900-1920) with documented multi-child household progression.

Source-Cited Facts

Birth/Death: Born 15 Dec 1859; died 8 Dec 1923 (age 63 years, 11 months, 23 days).
Maiden surname: Lewis; married name: Prior (married Arthur Edwin Prior c. 1900)
Burial: Brook Park Cemetery, Brook Park, Minnesota; inscription PRIOR / MARTHA E. / DEC. 15, 1859 / DEC. 8. 1923
The processed Prior timeline review places Martha in the direct line Wynant Williamson Lewis → Oliver Elhanon Lewis → Martha Eliza Lewis, which is useful branch placement but still a compiled-chart reading.

Census Records and Life Progression
1860 Wisconsin Census — Fond du Lac County, Ripon 1st Ward (as child)

Head: W.W. LEWIS, male, age 79, occupation farmer, born Massachusetts, property $50
Susan LEWIS (wife), female, age 58, born New York
Oliver LEWIS (son), male, age 35, born Vermont
Elizabeth LEWIS, female, age 25, born New York
Martha E. LEWIS (daughter), female, age 7/12 (infant), born Wisconsin
Source: Series M653, Roll 1408, Page 829; GSU microfilm available

1900 Minnesota Census — Mower County, Grand Meadows Township (as wife)

Head: Arthur PRIOR, male, race White, birthdate July 1853, age 46, born Michigan, occupation farmer
Martha PRIOR (wife), female, race White, birthdate Dec 1859, age 40, born Wisconsin
Children:

Oliver W PRIOR, male, race White, birthdate Mar 1880, age 20, born Minnesota, occupation farm laborer
George W PRIOR, male, race White, birthdate Aug 1883, age 16, born Minnesota, occupation farm laborer
Lewis H PRIOR, male, race White, birthdate Aug 1888, age 11, born Minnesota, occupation at school
Viva P PRIOR, female, race White, birthdate Nov 1895, age 4, born Minnesota
Lora P PRIOR, female, race White, birthdate Aug 1897, age 2, born Missouri


Source: Series T623, Roll 777, Page 180A; GSU microfilm available

1910 South Dakota Census — Brown County, Hanson Township

Head: Arthur PRIOR, male, race White, age 56, occupation laborer, birthplace Michigan
Martha PRIOR (wife), female, race White, age 50, occupation none
Children:

Lora PRIOR, female, race White, age 11
Arthur PRIOR (son), male, race White, age 7
Lewis PRIOR (son), male, race White, age 22, occupation laborer
Agnes PRIOR, female, race White, age 21, occupation none


Source: Series T624, Roll 392, Page 15B; GSU microfilm available

1920 Minnesota Census — Pine County, Pokegama Township

Head: Arthur E. PRIOR, male, race White, age 66, occupation farming
Martha E. PRIOR (wife), female, race White, age 60, occupation house keeping
Child:

Arthur E. PRIOR Jr, male, race White, age 16, occupation none


Source: Series T625, Roll 849, Pages 15A, ED 76; GSU microfilm available

Family Connections

Father: Oliver Warren Lewis (1823-1892), Vermont farmer
Mother: Martha Lewis (b. ~1801)
Husband: Arthur Edwin Prior (b. 1853 Michigan), farmer/laborer
Children identified: Oliver W., George W., Lewis H., Viva P., Lora P., Agnes, Arthur Jr. (7+ children across 40+ years)
Pedigree significance: Represents Lewis family extension into Minnesota/South Dakota through Prior marriage; agricultural worker family

Family Diagram

Locate Oliver Warren Lewis (father) in additional census records to establish full Lewis family lineage.
Verify Arthur Prior’s parentage and occupational transitions (farmer → laborer).
Trace all children’s adult lives in later records, especially sons Oliver W. and George W.
Clarify South Dakota relocation in 1910 (economic or family reasons).
Confirm return to Minnesota by 1920 and later life settlement.
Reconcile the chart’s Oliver Elhanon Lewis name form against existing Lewis-page naming.

People/Martha-Emblow.title: Martha Emblow
People/Martha-Emblow.content:
Martha Emblow
Biographical Profile

Name: Martha Emblow
Role in this project: Emblow-to-Kelly branch ancestor represented across 1841-1901 Peterborough census-summary entries.

Source-Cited Facts

A census-summary entry gives Martha Emblow as born about 1830 and died 21 Jul 1903.
The 1841 Peterborough entry places Martha Emblow in Joseph Emblow’s household.
The 1851 through 1901 entries track Martha Kelly in Peterborough households, including occupations tailoress/dressmaker and widow/head roles in later years.
The Bellamy pedigree timeline places Martha Emblow in the Bellamy collateral branch as the parent generation of Emma Kelly.
The processed Bellamy timeline review also keeps Martha Emblow in the Joseph Emblow branch and shows her as the key bridge into the Emma Kelly generation.

Research Gaps

Confirm Emblow-to-Kelly marital linkage from primary marriage records.
Validate incomplete RG10 fields in the 1871 segment from image-level records.
Reconcile child-name variants across 1861/1871/1881 entries.
Keep the chart-based Emma Kelly linkage as compiled support rather than a sole proof source.

People/Mary-Ann-Thorogood.title: Mary Ann Thorogood
People/Mary-Ann-Thorogood.content:
Mary Ann Thorogood
Biographical Profile

Name: Mary Ann Thorogood
Role in this project: Thorogood-line individual indexed in the census summary extraction.

Source-Cited Facts

A census-summary entry gives Mary Ann Thorogood as born 21 Mar 1832 and died 17 Jul 1916.
The extraction notes a UK census sequence for Mary Ann Thorogood from 1841 through 1911.
The Bellamy pedigree timeline places Mary Ann Thorogood in the Bellamy collateral branch through the Thorogood line.
The processed Bellamy timeline review adds an important chart note: James Thorogood and Mary Ann Thorogood are explicitly labeled as brother and sister.
The Burial Sites book places Mary Ann Thorogood at Felsted Congregational Burial Ground (United Reformed Church) in Felsted, Essex, England (page 41), with inscription IN MEMORY OF / OUR DEAR MOTHER / MARY ANN MUNSON / WHO DIED 17th JULY 1916 / AGED 84 YEARS. The page also carries an OCR date-of-death field that appears inconsistent and should be treated cautiously. Map: Google Maps.

Research Gaps

Confirm maiden name and relationship structure across census years.
Validate the July 1916 death record against civil-registration indexes.
Keep the sibling note visible while the exact Thorogood family structure remains secondary-source-heavy.

People/Mary-Burgett.title: Mary Burgett
People/Mary-Burgett.content:
Mary Burgett
Biographical Profile

Name: Mary Burgett
Role in this project: Burgett-to-Spicer line ancestor represented across Iowa census-summary entries.

Source-Cited Facts

A census-summary entry gives Mary Burgett as born 19 Sep 1835 and died 28 Jan 1918.
The section includes 1850 Brush Run Township, Iowa County, Iowa household records for the Bergett family.
The same source batch also contains a separate 1850 Iowa mortality schedule entry for Mary BERGETT that is tracked as Mary Greenwood; it is a different person from this Mary Burgett because the mortality entry gives age 38 and a March 1850 death.
Later entries in the same section include Mary Spicer in Linn County, Iowa households, including 1900 Clinton Township (Mary SPICER, relation mother) and 1910 Maine Township (Mary SPICER, relation mother-in-law in a Duffield household).
The Burial Sites book lists Mary Burgett as page 11 and places her in the Spicer/Spooner Cemetery section (page 52), which also includes George B. Spicer and Hattie May Risden Spicer. Map: Google Maps.
The processed Spicer timeline review confirms Mary Burgett as the spouse line paired with Charles Russell Spicer and keeps Mary Greenwood separate as an older Burgett-side figure.

Family Diagram

graph TD
  WB[William Burgett<br/>c1807-c1850]
  MB[Mary Burgett<br/>1835-1918]
  GB[George B. Spicer]
  HMR[Hattie May Risden Spicer]
  MGS[Mary Greenwood / Mary Bergett<br/>separate mortality record]

  WB --- MB
  MB --> GB
  GB --- HMR
  MGS -. separate identity .- WB

This is a compact relationship sketch. The dashed link to Mary Greenwood is only a reminder that the mortality record is separate, not a genealogical merge.
Research Gaps

Confirm continuity between Bergett/Burgett forms and later Spicer household entries.
Validate all listed relationships from image-level census pages.
Reconcile age/birth-year drift across listed decades.
Keep Mary Greenwood separate from Mary Burgett in all later branch summaries and diagrams.

People/Mary-Greenwood.title: Mary Greenwood
People/Mary-Greenwood.content:
Mary Greenwood
Biographical Profile

Name: Mary Greenwood
Role in this project: Burgett-linked individual represented in 1850 Iowa mortality schedule summary entry.

Source-Cited Facts

The extracted census summary page 30 labels this profile GREENWOOD, Mary (c. 1812 - Mar 1850).
The same extract records an 1850 Iowa mortality schedule entry naming Mary BERGETT, female, age 38, born England.
The entry notes death in March with cause recorded as fever and illness duration of 3 days.
The extract cites Series: A1156, Roll: 54, Page: 337, Line #6.
The same source page flags this as a Burgett-linked individual, but the age and death timing make it a separate Mary Bergett identity from Mary Burgett (1835-1918).
The GREENWOOD, Mary heading appears to be compiler metadata for the mortality-schedule row, not the best canonical surname for the person.

Research Gaps

Validate mortality-schedule line details from image-level records.
Determine whether the GREENWOOD heading should be preserved only as an index label or replaced by a more neutral mortality-record title.
Clarify the relationship of this Mary Bergett entry to the William Burgett household.

Role in this project: Sorrell-line individual indexed in the census summary extraction.

Source-Cited Facts

A census-summary entry gives Mary Sorrell as born about 1823 and died 20 Jun 1897.
The extraction lists a UK sequence for Mary Sorrell from 1841 through 1891 across Essex and Hertfordshire entries.
The Burial Sites book index lists Mary Sorrell as c1827-1897, but the extracted text did not reveal a standalone cemetery page.
The Bellamy pedigree timeline includes Mary Sorrell in the Bellamy collateral branch as the daughter linked to the later Sorrell line.
The processed Bellamy timeline review keeps that parentage as a chart-layout reading under the later James Sorrell branch rather than a resolved proof statement.

Mary Woodley
Biographical Profile

Name: Mary Woodley
Role in this project: Harrison-line mother of Elizabeth Harrison, connecting the Tallman branch to the Rockingham County Harrison/Woodley material in Settlers by the Long Grey Trail.

Source-Cited Facts

Settlers by the Long Grey Trail page 312 says Nathaniel Harrison married Mary Woodley in 1784 and gives her birth date as 18 February 1764.
Page 374 identifies Mary as a daughter of Jacob Woodley.
Page 375 identifies Jacob Woodley’s wife as Grace Looker and lists Mary, also called Polly, among their children.
Page 375 says Mary married Nathaniel Harrison.

People/Mathias-Miller.title: Mathias Miller
People/Mathias-Miller.content:
Mathias Miller
Biographical Profile

Name: Mathias Miller
Role in this project: Miller-line household head represented in 1850-1870 Walnut Township, Ohio census-summary entries.

Source-Cited Facts

A census-summary heading records Mathias Miller with dates 7 Jan 1879 - 4 May 1871, which appear to contain a birth-year transcription error.
The profile section includes 1850, 1860, and 1870 Walnut Township, Fairfield County, Ohio household tables with Mathias Miller and family members.
The extracts cite M432 Roll 677 Page 197 (1850), M653 Roll 961 Page 247 (1860), and M593 Roll 1198 Page 387B (1870).
The Genealogy and History of Samuel Miller pages 11-15 present an older compiled Miller chain from Samuel Miller to John Miller.
Page 240 identifies Mathias Miller as a child of John Miller and Anna Smootz.
The Genealogy and History of Samuel Miller page image for page 240 gives Mathias Miller as born 7 Jan 1789 and died 4 May 1871.
The same page says Mathias Miller was born in Rockingham County, Virginia; moved with his parents to Fairfield County, Ohio, about 1805; married Amy E. Critton about 1818; owned land in and around Millersport; was a founder of the town; and was a contractor in digging the Ohio Canal.

Family Connections

Father: John Miller, based on the compiled Miller genealogy.
Grandfather: Samuel Miller, based on the compiled Miller genealogy.
Child: Romancy Miller.

Research Gaps

Confirm the corrected 1789 birth year and 1871 death date with cemetery, probate, or other primary records.
Validate all household members and occupations from image-level census pages.
Determine whether duplicate Mathias Miller entries in 1870 reflect father/son disambiguation.
Verify the older Samuel-to-John-to-Mathias chain in primary land, probate, or church records.

People/Matilda-Baxter.title: Matilda Baxter
People/Matilda-Baxter.content:
Matilda Baxter
Biographical Profile

Name: Matilda Baxter
Role in this project: Rowland-line ancestor represented in 1850-1870 US census-summary household extracts.

Source-Cited Facts

A census-summary entry gives Matilda Baxter as born 31 Mar 1818 (uncertain marker in summary) and died 1 Apr 1879.
The 1850 Graham Township, Jefferson County, Indiana household lists Matilda Rowland with Jesse Rowland and children.
The 1860 Waterford Township, Clinton County, Iowa household lists Matilda Roland in the Jesse Roland household.
The 1870 Deep Creek Township, Clinton County, Iowa household includes Jesse Rowland, Matilda Rowland, and Martha Rowland.
The Burial Sites book places Matilda Baxter at Rossiter Cemetery south of Charlotte, Iowa (page 6), E56 and 330th Avenue, Washington Township, Clinton County, Iowa, with Lat/Long 41°56’31.3”N 90°27’43.3”W and inscription fragments indicating MATILDA and APRIL 1, 1879 / AGED 61 YEARS, 1 DAY / WIFE OF J.B. / ROWLAND. Map: Google Maps.

                  Priority Research Leads

Matilda Hiskey
Biographical Profile

Name: Matilda Hiskey
Role in this project: Monson-linked ancestor represented in 1841 Essex census-summary extraction.

Source-Cited Facts

A census-summary entry gives Matilda Hiskey as born about 1787 and died 3 May 1849.
The 1841 Great Holland, Essex entry (In the Green) lists Matilda Monson in a household with Arther Monson and children/younger members.
The compiled entry references HO107 class metadata with incomplete piece/folio/page fields.

Research Gaps

Confirm Hiskey-to-Monson linkage from marriage/parish records.
Validate all names and ages in the 1841 household from image-level census page.
Reconcile spelling variants (Arther/Arthur, Monson/Munson).

People/May-Aleen-Palmer.title: May Aleen Palmer
People/May-Aleen-Palmer.content:
May Aleen Palmer
Biographical Profile

Name: May Aleen Palmer (later May Aleen Prior)
Role in this project: Palmer-to-Prior branch matriarch spanning Minnesota (1900), Iowa (1910-1930) with documented multi-child household progression.

Source-Cited Facts

Birth/Death: Born 1 May 1886; died 21 May 1979 (age 93 years, 20 days).
Maiden surname: Palmer; married name: Prior (married Oliver Prior c. 1910)
Burial: Cedar Memorial Cemetery, Cedar Rapids, Iowa; 110 Lakeview, Space 5; GPS 42°1’25.1”N 91°37’47.2”W; inscription OUR MOM / MAY A. PRIOR / MAY 1, 1886 / MAY 21, 1979
The processed Prior timeline review confirms May Aleen Palmer as the direct spouse line attached to Oliver Warren Prior and preserves her chart ID as a search lead only.

Census Records and Life Progression
1900 Minnesota Census — Mower County, Frankford Township (as daughter)

Head: William PALMER, male, farmer, age 49
Elizabeth PALMER (mother), age 42
May PALMER (daughter), female, birthdate May 1887, age 13, born Wisconsin
Siblings in household: Vern, Louis, Dott, Ivas, Voila, Vivian
Note: Listed as age 13, born Wisconsin (variance from 1886 birth year); suggests May 1887 birth in census vs. May 1886 reported in summary index
Source: Series T623, Roll 777, Page 3A; GSU microfilm available

1910 Iowa Census — Blackhawk County, East Waterloo Township, Waterloo

Head: Oliver PRIOR, male, race White, age 30, occupation moulder foundry
Wife: May PRIOR, female, race White, age 24
Child:

Martha PRIOR, female, race White, age 5, occupation none


Source: Series T624, Roll 392, Page 226; GSU microfilm available

1920 Iowa Census — Linn County, Cedar Rapids, 275 12th Ave E

Head: Oliver PRIOR, male, race White, age 39, occupation moulder
Wife: May PRIOR, female, race White, age 33, no occupation
Children:

Martha PRIOR, female, age 15, no occupation
Everett PRIOR, male, age 12, no occupation
Viola PRIOR, female, age 9, no occupation
Viola PRIOR, female, age 9, no occupation (duplicate entry or OCR error?)
Ruby PRIOR, female, age 6, no occupation
Ronald PRIOR, male, age 4+, no occupation
Vergil PRIOR, male, age 1+, no occupation
Maxine PRIOR, female, age 11 months, no occupation


Source: Series T625, Roll 500, Pages 3B, ED 131; GSU microfilm available

1930 Iowa Census — Linn County, Cedar Rapids, 14th Precinct, RFD #4 Memorial Drive

Head: Oliver PRIOR, male, race White, age 50, occupation moulder foundry
Wife: May A PRIOR, female, race White, age 43, no occupation
Children and Grandchildren:

Martha E CASHMAN, female, age 25, married, operator telephone
Warren A CASHMAN, male, age 3+, no occupation
Beverly M CASHMAN, female, age 2+, no occupation
Bruce F CASHMAN, male, age 8 months, no occupation
Everett ? PRIOR, male, age 22, no occupation, core maker foundry
Viola D PRIOR, female, age 19, no occupation, mangle operator laundry
Ruby B PRIOR, female, age 16, no occupation, mangle operator laundry
Ronald W PRIOR, male, age 14, no occupation, none
Vergil V PRIOR, male, age 12, no occupation, none
Maxine M PRIOR, female, age 10, no occupation, none
La Verne O PRIOR, male, age 8, no occupation, none
Oliver W PRIOR Jr, male, age 3+, no occupation, none
Clint R PRIOR, male, age 2, no occupation, none


Note: Large household with oldest child Martha married with children; occupational diversity (foundry work, telephone operator, laundry workers)
Source: Series T626, Roll 665, Page 26B, ED 57; GSU microfilm available

Family Connections

Father: William Henry Palmer (1851-1927), Minnesota farmer
Grandfather: John K Palmer (1821-1906), Wisconsin farmer
Husband: Oliver Prior (married c. 1910), foundry moulder
Children identified: Martha E., Everett, Viola, Ruby, Ronald, Vergil, Maxine, La Verne, Oliver Jr., Clint (10+ children/stepchildren across three decades)
Pedigree significance: Represents Palmer-Prior marriage alliance and family expansion into Iowa industrial economy; bridge between Wisconsin farming (grandfather/father) and Iowa foundry/industrial labor (husband and children)

Family Diagram

graph TD
  WHP[“William Henry Palmer<br/>1851-1927<br/>Minnesota farmer”]
  MAP[“May Aleen Palmer<br/>1886-1979”]
  OP[“Oliver Prior<br/>Foundry moulder”]

  OP --> M
  OP --> EV
  OP --> V
  OP --> R
  OP --> RO
  OP --> VE
  OP --> MA
  OP --> LV
  OP --> OJ
  OP --> C

May Aleen Palmer’s life arc spans Minnesota farm childhood (1900) through Iowa industrial marriage (1910+) to large multigenerational family (1920-1930), representing occupational transition from agriculture to urban foundry work.
Research Gaps

Clarify birth year discrepancy: 1886 (burial inscription/summary) vs. 1887 (1900 census).
Validate all children names and birth years from original 1920/1930 census images (duplicate Viola entries suggest OCR errors).
Identify Martha’s husband surname (Cashman) and trace his line.
Trace remaining children’s adult lives in later records.
Confirm Oliver Prior’s occupational and family details.
Keep the chart’s alphanumeric ID as a lead only rather than a verified external-profile match.

People/Miller-Mathias-Tallman.title: Miller Mathias Tallman
People/Miller-Mathias-Tallman.content:
Miller Mathias Tallman
Biographical Profile

Name: Miller Mathias Tallman
Role in this project: Tallman-line ancestor connected to Ault-line census households.

Source-Cited Facts

Birth/Death: Born 14 Apr 1841; died 8 Apr 1921 (age 79 years, 11 months, 25 days).
Birthplace: Ohio
Occupations: Farmer (1880), teamster (1900)
Military Service: Iowa Soldiers’ Home resident (1910-1920), suggesting Union veteran status
Burial: Iowa Veterans’ Home Cemetery, Marshalltown, Iowa; South Section, Row 12, Grave 4
The Genealogy and History of Samuel Miller page image for page 241 identifies Miller Mathias Tallman as a child of Romancy Miller and Benjamin Tallman and gives his birth date as 14 Apr 1841.

Census Records and Life Progression
1880 Iowa Census — Cherokee County, Afton Township (as farmer)

Head: Miller M. TALLMAN, male, married, age 39, occupation farmer, born Ohio
Wife: Elizabeth TALLMAN (née Ault), married, age 30, born Ohio, occupation keeping house
Children:

Willis G. TALLMAN, male, single, age 6, born Iowa
Lena TALLMAN, female, single, age 1, born Iowa


Household also includes:

Elbert WINN, male, single, age 17, born Iowa
Mary AULT (Elizabeth’s mother), female, widow, age 73, born Maine, occupation aged
Andrew RICHEY, male, single, age 24, born Illinois, occupation farm laborer


Source: Fam Hist Lib Film 1254332, Page 52D; GSU microfilm available

1900 Iowa Census — Woodbury County, Sioux City, p. 231R, Filmore Avenue (as teamster)

Head: Miller M. TALLMAN, male, race White, birthdate Apr 1841, age 58, occupation teamster
Wife: Elizabeth P. TALLMAN (née Ault), race White, birthdate Oct 1841, age 58
Child:

Lena P. TALLMAN, female, race White, birthdate Feb 1879, age 21


Note: Household in Sioux City; occupation changed from farmer to teamster (urban/transport labor)
Source: Series T623, Roll 467, Page 231B; GSU microfilm available

1910 Iowa Census — Marshall County, Linn Township, Iowa Soldiers’ Home

Head: Miller M. TALLMAN, male, race White, age 69, occupation none
Wife: Elizabeth P. TALLMAN, female, race White, age 68
Note: Residing at Iowa Soldiers’ Home; both listed as members
Source: Series T624, Roll 441, Page 72; GSU microfilm available

1920 Iowa Census — Marshall County, Iowa Soldiers’ Home

Head: Miller M. TALLMAN, male, race White, age 78, occupation none, member of Soldiers’ Home
Wife: Elizabeth TALLMAN, female, race White, age 78, member of Soldiers’ Home
Note: Continued residence at Iowa Soldiers’ Home; married couple both identified as members
Source: Series T625, Roll 502, Pages 5B, ED 155; GSU microfilm available

Family Connections

Wife: Elizabeth Plomey Ault (1841-1926), married c. 1880
Children identified: Willis G. Tallman (b. ~1874), Lena/Lena P. Tallman (b. 1879)
Mother-in-law: Mary Ault (b. ~1802), living with household in 1880
Pedigree connection: Married into Frederick Ault’s family; likely Union veteran based on Iowa Soldiers’ Home residence
Compiled-book reconciliation issue: The Miller genealogy page 242 says Mathias M. Tallman married Lizzie Wimmer on 24 Aug 1872 and lists children William Gilmore Tallman and Elvin Lenora Tallman. The same page also summarizes the children as Willis G. Tallman and Lenore Tallman; page 250 identifies Elvin Lenora Tallman as wife of U. B. Thorpe. These details likely correspond to the census-linked Elizabeth/Willis/Lena family group, but marriage and vital records are still needed before treating Lizzie Wimmer as resolved to Elizabeth Plomey Ault.

Family Diagram

graph TD
  MMT["Miller Mathias Tallman<br/>1841-1921<br/>Farmer to Teamster to Veteran"]
  EPA["Elizabeth Plomey Ault<br/>1841-1926"]
  
  WG["Willis G. Tallman<br/>b. ~1874"]
  LT["Lenore Hetty Tallman<br/>Elvin Lenora?<br/>1879-1953"]
  
  MMT --- EPA
  MMT --> WG
  MMT --> LT

Miller Mathias demonstrates the trajectory of a Civil War veteran: farming in rural Iowa (1880), transitioning to urban teamster work in Sioux City (1900), and finally entering the Iowa Soldiers’ Home for long-term care (1910-1920) until his death in 1921.
Research Gaps


                  
                  Priority Research Leads 
                  
                

The following census records are indicated in the pedigree diagrams but matching transcripts are missing from the vault:

1890 Census: Transcript needed to verify household context. (Note: The 1890 US Federal Census was largely destroyed by fire)
1910 Census: Transcript needed to verify household context.

People/Nathan-Spicer.title: Nathan Spicer
People/Nathan-Spicer.content:
Nathan Spicer
Biographical Profile

Name: Nathan Spicer
Birth/Death: Born 3 Apr 1796; died 16 May 1873 (age 77 years, 1 month, 13 days)
Role in this project: Spicer line patriarch; grandfather of George B. Spicer; patriarch of Iowa Spicer agricultural line (1850s-1870s).
Burial: Spicer/Spooner Cemetery, Homestead, Iowa; Grave 13 on Highway 6, 1.5 miles east; inscription NATHAN SPICER / DIED / May 16, 1873 / AGED / 77yrs. 1mo. 13ds; Burial Sites book, page 34.
The processed Spicer timeline review places this Nathan after two earlier Nathan Spicer labels and before the unresolved repeated-Nathan / Claramon Tiffany zone.

Census Records and Household Context
1850 Iowa Census — Johnson County, Iowa City, District 9

Head: Henry Usher, male, age 37, blacksmith, born New York
Wife: Maria Usher, female, age 36, born New York (Nathan’s daughter)
Children: Henry Usher Jr., age 9; Martha Farbanks, age 6 (both Iowa)
Related: Nathaniel Spicer, male, age 54, blacksmith, born New York (Nathan — patriarch)
Other family: Henry Spicer, male, age 20, Ohio; Zura Spicer, female, age 18, Ohio (likely Nathan’s children)
Note: Nathan appears in daughter Maria’s household as aging patriarch
Source: Series M432, Roll 185, Page 148

1860 Iowa Census — Iowa County, Iowa Township

Head: Wm Spicer, male, age 42, farmer, born New York (Nathan’s son William)
Wife: Deliha Spicer, female, age 43, born Ohio
Children: Mary C. Spicer, female, age 6, Iowa
Nathan Spicer, male, age 64, farm laborer, born New York (aging patriarch, now dependent)
Other: Wm Ferguson, male, age 55, farm laborer, born Ohio
Source: Series M653, Roll 325, Page 628

1870 Iowa Census — Iowa County, Amana Township

Head: William Spicer, male, age 54, farmer, born New York
Wife: Delilah Spicer, female, age 58, keeping house, born Ohio
Nathan Spicer, male, age 74, listed as “without prospect” (no occupation), born New York
Note: Nathan now elderly (74) in son William’s household; no occupation listed
Property: William’s farm valued at 1500; personal property 600
Source: Series M593, Roll 396, Page 259

Family Connections


Children identified:

Maria Usher (daughter, married Henry Usher, blacksmith)
Henry Spicer (son, age 20 in 1850, Ohio-born)
Zura Spicer (daughter, age 18 in 1850, Ohio-born)
William Spicer (son, major farmer with 1500 acres in 1860-1870)
Charles Russell Spicer (son? — lineage suggests Nathan → Charles Russell → George B.)



Son William Spicer: Farmer, estate valued $1500, supports aging father in 1860s


Grandchildren: Through William and Delilah: Mary C. Spicer visible in 1860


Household trajectory:

1850: Aging patriarch (54) visiting daughter Maria’s blacksmith household
1860: Dependent in son William’s farming household (age 64, farm laborer)
1870: Fully dependent elderly (age 74, “without prospect”) in son’s household



Burial and Legacy

Burial: Spicer/Spooner Cemetery, Homestead, Iowa (same cemetery where George B. Spicer’s wife Hattie May Risden was eventually buried)
Legacy: Patriarch of Spicer line spanning 77 years (1796-1873); established in Iowa by 1850; sons continued agricultural prosperity (William with 1500-acre farm by 1860)

Three-Generation Spicer Progression

Nathan Spicer (1796-1873): Patriarch, emigrated from New York/Ohio; established in Iowa by 1850
Charles Russell Spicer (1822-1887): Son; farmer, chair maker, bridge generation
George B. Spicer (1864-1938): Grandson; major patriarch with large household, 7+ children; prosperous farmer

Research Gaps

Confirm spouse name(s) for Nathan — appears unnamed in census summaries
Clarify exact lineage: Nathan → Charles Russell → George B. (son→son or other relationship?)
Verify death date and location from vital records (Homestead burial suggests Iowa County residence)
Explore Henry and Zura Spicer (children, Ohio-born, date/context unclear)
Keep the repeated-Nathan chart problem explicit instead of flattening it into a single solved line.

People/Nathaniel-Harrison.title: Nathaniel Harrison
People/Nathaniel-Harrison.content:
Nathaniel Harrison
Biographical Profile

Name: Nathaniel Harrison
Role in this project: Harrison-line father of Elizabeth Harrison, whose marriage to John Tallman connects the Harrison material to the Tallman branch.

Source-Cited Facts

Settlers by the Long Grey Trail page 312 lists Nathaniel Harrison as a child of Capt. Reuben Harrison and first wife Lydia Harrison.
Page 312 says Nathaniel Harrison died in 1807 and married Mary Woodley in 1784.
Page 375 says Nathaniel Harrison died testate in 1807 and left no widow, but left children named Lydia, Phebe, Gracy, Anna, Elizabeth, Mary, Jerusha, Edith, Lucretia, Samuel, and Nathaniel.
Page 375 says litigation over Nathaniel Harrison’s heirs identified Elizabeth as wife of John Tallman.

Family Connections

Spouse: Mary Woodley
Child in this project: Elizabeth Harrison
Son-in-law: John Tallman

Research Gaps

Locate Nathaniel Harrison’s original 1807 probate or estate records.
Verify the Burkholder litigation cited in the compiled book.
Confirm the full child list against primary records before extending descendant pages.

Sources

Book Outprints — Settlers by the Long Grey Trail
Settlers by the Long Grey Trail Extraction Index

People/Oliver-Warren-Lewis.title: Oliver Warren Lewis
People/Oliver-Warren-Lewis.content:
Oliver Warren Lewis
Biographical Profile

Name: Oliver Warren Lewis
Role in this project: Lewis-line patriarch spanning Vermont origin through Wisconsin and Minnesota (1850-1880s) with multi-generational household progression.

Source-Cited Facts

Birth/Death: Born 13 Aug 1823; died 3 Jul 1892 (age 68 years, 10 months, 20 days).
Birthplace: Vermont
Occupation: Farmer

Census Records and Household Context
1850 Wisconsin Census — Racine County, Town of Burlington

Head: Wynat LEWIS (possibly William?), male, age 68, occupation farmer, born Vermont
Susan LEWIS (wife), female, age 54, born Vermont
Children/relatives in household:

Eliza LEWIS, female, age 37, born Vermont
Alonzo LEWIS, male, age 33, occupation farmer, born Vermont
Oliver LEWIS, male, age 25, occupation farmer, born Vermont
Marth LEWIS, female, age 19, born Vermont
Erastus DARLING, male, age 15, born Vermont
Sarah DARLING, female, age 12, born Vermont


Source: Series M432, Roll 1004, Page 150; GSU microfilm available

1860 Wisconsin Census — Fond du Lac County, Ripon 1st Ward

Head: W.W. LEWIS, male, age 79, occupation farmer, born Massachusetts, property $50
Susan LEWIS (wife), female, age 58, born New York
Oliver LEWIS (son), male, age 35, born Vermont
Marshal LEWIS, male, age 25, born Vermont
Elizabeth LEWIS, female, age 24, born New York
Martha E. LEWIS (daughter), female, age 7/12 (infant), born Wisconsin
Source: Series M653, Roll 1408, Page 829; GSU microfilm available

1880 Minnesota Census — Mower County, Grand Meadow Township

Head: Oliver LEWIS, male, self, married, age 57, born Vermont, occupation farmer
Elizabeth LEWIS (wife), female, married, age 44, born New York, occupation keeping house
Children:

George LEWIS, male, single, age 16, born Wisconsin, occupation working on farm
William LEWIS, male, single, age 9, born Minnesota, occupation at school


Source: Fam Hist Lib Film 1254626; GSU microfilm available

Family Connections

Wife: Susan Lewis (b. ~1796 Vermont/New York) in 1850; Elizabeth Lewis (b. ~1836 New York) in 1880
Children identified: Eliza (b. ~1813), Alonzo (b. ~1817), Oliver (b. 1823), Martha/Marth (b. ~1831); later children George (b. ~1864), William (b. ~1871)
Daughter: Martha Eliza Lewis (1859-1923), who married Arthur Prior
Pedigree significance: Vermont-origin farmer establishing Lewis line in Wisconsin (1850) and expanding to Minnesota (1880)

Family Diagram

graph TD

  OWL --> MEL

Oliver Warren Lewis represents the Vermont-origin farmer patriarch, expanding the Lewis family across Wisconsin and Minnesota with documented multi-generational household presence (1850-1880).
Research Gaps

Clarify relationship between Oliver Warren Lewis (b. 1823) and Wynat/William Lewis (b. 1768, head of 1850 household). Possible father-son relationship.
Validate all children names and relationships from original 1850-1880 census images.
Determine reason for dual wife names (Susan vs. Elizabeth) across decades—possible remarriage or OCR error.
Trace children Eliza, Alonzo, and Martha beyond 1850 records.
Confirm death date and burial location.

People/Oliver-Warren-Prior.title: Oliver Warren Prior
People/Oliver-Warren-Prior.content:
Oliver Warren Prior
Biographical Profile

Name: Oliver Warren Prior
Role in this project: Prior branch household head represented in 1900-1930 census-summary entries.

Source-Cited Facts

A census-summary entry gives Oliver Warren Prior as born 15 Mar 1880 and died 29 May 1949.
The 1900 Grand Meadows Township, Mower County, Minnesota entry lists Oliver W. Prior as son in Arthur and Martha Prior’s household.
The 1910 East Waterloo Township, 1920 Cedar Rapids, and 1930 Cedar Rapids entries track Oliver and May Prior households with multiple children.
The Prior pedigree timeline places Oliver Warren Prior (1880-1949) in the generation following Arthur Edwin Prior and Martha Eliza Lewis.
The processed Prior timeline review also keeps Oliver as the parent generation immediately above Ruby Bernice Prior.
The Burial Sites book places Oliver Warren Prior at Cedar Memorial Cemetery in Cedar Rapids, Iowa (page 26), 110 Lakeview, Space 4, with GPS coordinates 42°1’25.1”N 91°37’47.2”W, date of death 30 May 1949, and inscription OUR DAD / OLIVER W. PRIOR / MARCH 15, 1880 / MAY 29, 1949. Map: Google Maps.

Research Gaps

Resolve OCR ambiguity in child names and sex markers in 1920/1930 entries.
Confirm continuity with foundry/moulder occupation across decades.
Validate death date from independent records.
Keep the chart-based parentage link to Ruby Bernice Prior tied to the broader census and burial context.

People/Patience-Durfee-Tallman.content:
Patience Durfee Tallman
Biographical Profile

Name: Patience Durfee Tallman
Role in this project: Durfee-line spouse connecting the older Tallman line to The Descendants of Thomas Durfee.

Source-Cited Facts

The Descendants of Thomas Durfee volume I page 31 says Patience Durfee was a daughter of Thomas Durfee and his second wife, Deliverance Hall/Tripp Durfee.
The same page says she was born in Portsmouth, Rhode Island, and died in Portsmouth in 1723.
The same page says she married Benjamin Tallman at Portsmouth, Rhode Island, on 23 September 1708.

Family Connections

Spouse: Benjamin Tallman 1684-1769
Child in this project: William Tallman

Research Gaps

Verify Patience’s birth, marriage, and death from Portsmouth, Rhode Island records.
Confirm the Durfee/Hall/Tripp parentage from primary records.

People/Rebecca-Blake.title: Rebecca Blake
People/Rebecca-Blake.content:
Rebecca Blake
Biographical Profile

Name: Rebecca Blake
Role in this project: Lemmon-linked ancestor represented in 1850 Ohio census-summary household extract.

Source-Cited Facts

Birth/Death: Born 26 Oct 1779; died 29 Mar 1855 (age 75 years, 5 months, 3 days per burial record).
Burial: Tew Cemetery, Townsend Township, Sandusky County, Ohio; Section 25-17-5, Coordinates 412203N 0825125W; inscription reads REBECCA / wife of / JAMES LEMMON / DIED / Mar. 29, 1855 / Aged / 75 Yrs. 5.M. 3d; Burial Sites book, page 9.
Spouse: James Lemmon (1779-1854); married name: Lemmon
The Sandusky-Beers sketch for John McIntyre Lemmon states that James Lemmon Sr. married Rebecca Blake in 1805 and identifies them as John McIntyre Lemmon’s grandparents.

Census Records and Household Context
1850 Ohio Census — Sandusky County, Townsend Township

Head: James Lemmon, male, age 71, farmer, born Pennsylvania
Rebecca Lemmon (spouse), female, age 71, born Pennsylvania
Household composition:

Mathew? Lemmon, male, age 38, farmer, born New York (son)
Sarah Lemmon, female, age 21, born New York (daughter) — later Sarah Annett Lemmon
Frank Lemmon, male, age 1, born Ohio (grandson or younger son)
Nathan Harkins, male, age 12, born New York (boarder/relative)
David Lemmon, male, age 21, farmer, born New York (son or relative)


Source: Series M432, Roll 726, Page 476, R/F 1173/1198; GSU microfilm available

Family Connections

Husband: James Lemmon (b. 1779 Pennsylvania) — farmer, patriarch of Ohio Lemmon line
Children identified: Uriah Blake Lemmon from the Sandusky-Beers sketch, plus Matthew, Sarah (later Annett), Frank, and David from census extraction where exact relationships still need image-level confirmation.
Grandson: John McIntyre Lemmon
Age at 1850 census: 71 years old (born 1779)
Pedigree significance: Links the Blake surname to Lemmon family; appears on same timeline chart as Uriah Blake Lemmon and Uriah Blake Thorpe
Burial location: Same cemetery (Tew) as husband James Lemmon, adjacent plots in same section

Family Diagram

graph TD
  RB["Rebecca Blake<br/>1779-1855"]
  JL["James Lemmon<br/>1779-1854"]
  
  MT["Matthew<br/>Farmer"]
  SA["Sarah A.<br/>→ Monroe Thorpe"]
  FL["Frank"]
  DL["David<br/>Farmer"]
  
  RB --- JL
  JL --> MT
  JL --> SA
  JL --> FL
  JL --> DL

Rebecca Blake married James Lemmon and anchors the Blake-Lemmon connection; their daughter Sarah Annett carried the Lemmon line forward into the Thorpe branch.

1840 Census: Transcript needed to verify household context.
1860 Census: Transcript needed to verify household context.

People/Richard-Bellamy.title: Richard Bellamy
People/Richard-Bellamy.content:
Richard Bellamy
Biographical Profile

Name: Richard Bellamy
Role in this project: Core Bellamy-line ancestor with repeated 1841-1881 Lincolnshire census-summary entries.

Source-Cited Facts

A census-summary entry gives Richard Bellamy as baptized 18 Mar 1816 and died 24 May 1902.
The 1841 Bourn entry includes Richard Bellamy with James Bellamy at Castle Yard.
The 1851, 1861, and 1871 Bourn entries list Richard Bellamy as household head with wife Mary and children including William, Thomas, George, Ann, Henry, and Alice across different years.
The 1871 entry is explicitly cited in census-citation notes as RG10 Piece 3314, Folio 65, Page 5.
The Bellamy pedigree timeline places Richard Bellamy in the Bellamy branch immediately after James Bellamy.
The processed Bellamy timeline review confirms Richard as the bridge between James Bellamy and Henry James Bellamy; adjacent spouse placements such as Rebecca Culpin and Mary Whitfield remain chart-layout readings.

Research Gaps

Validate all household-member carryover across 1851-1881 entries from source images.
Resolve spelling variants for birthplace strings (Bourn/Bourne, Hanthorpe/Manthorpe).
Add occupation chronology from full table extraction.
Keep the early Bellamy parentage above Richard open until stronger evidence resolves it.

Robert “Butch” Thorpe
Biographical Profile

People/Romancy-Miller.title: Romancy Miller
People/Romancy-Miller.content:
Romancy Miller
Biographical Profile

Name: Romancy Miller (later Romancy Tallman)
Role in this project: Tallman-line matriarch spanning Iowa (1850-1910) with six-decade household documentation.

Source-Cited Facts

Birth/Death: Born 10 Apr 1819; died 31 Jan 1911 (age 91 years, 8 months, 21 days).
Maiden surname: Miller; married name: Tallman (married Benjamin Tallman c. 1840s)
The Genealogy and History of Samuel Miller page image for page 240 identifies Romancy Miller as a child of Mathias Miller and Amy E. Critton.
Page 240-241 says Romancy Miller married Benjamin F. Tallman on 25 Oct 1834 at Millersport, Ohio; moved to Canal Winchester, Ohio, about 1845; moved to Jones County, Iowa, in 1848; later lived in Rippey, Greene County, Iowa; and was affiliated with the M.E. Church.

Census Records and Life Progression
1850 Iowa Census — Jones County, Rome Township

Head: Benjamin TOLLMAN, male, age 39, occupation farmer, born Ohio
Romancy TOLLMAN (wife), female, age 32, born Ohio
Children in household:

Miller TOLLMAN, male, age 9, born Ohio
Emma E TOLLMAN, female, age 5, born Ohio
Sarah J TOLLMAN, female, age 14, born Ohio
Eliza E TOLLMAN, female, age 3, born Ohio
Nathaniel H TOLLMAN, male, age 2, born Iowa


Household also includes:

John VANOISDELL?, male, age 20, occupation farm hand
Elizabeth McVETE?, female, age 33, occupation domestic
Matthew BOWER, male, age 60


Source: Series M432, Roll 185, Page 192; GSU microfilm available

1860 Iowa Census — Linn County, College Township, Western

Head: Benjamin TALMON, male, age 47, occupation farmer, born Ohio, property $5,000
Romcy TALMON (wife), female, age 40, born Ohio
Children:

Miller TALMON, male, age 19, born Ohio, occupation farmer
Ama TALMON, female, age 16, born Ohio
Eliza E TALMON, female, age 13, born Ohio
Nathaniel TALMON, male, age 11, born Iowa
Ama E TALMON, female, age 6, born Iowa
Griffin C TALMON, male, age 7, born Iowa
William L TALMON, male, age 2, born Iowa


Source: Series 653, Roll 532, Page 387; GSU microfilm available

1870 Iowa Census — Linn County, College Township, Page 180

Head: Benj TALLMAN, male, age 59, occupation farmer, born Ohio
Romancee TALLMAN (wife), female, age 56, born Ohio
Children:

Matt H TALLMAN, male, age 28, born Ohio, occupation farmer
Nathan H TALLMAN, male, age 21, born Iowa, occupation farm laborer
Griffin C TALLMAN, male, age 18, born Iowa, occupation farm laborer
Romancee TALLMAN, female, age 14, born Iowa
Wm L TALLMAN, male, age 8, born Iowa
Jacob M TALLMAN, male, age 5, born Iowa


Note: Mathias notation possibly indicating additional children at top of page
Source: Series M593, Roll 405, Page 180; GSU microfilm available

1880 Iowa Census — Linn County, College Township, Western

Head: B. TALLMAN (Benjamin), male, self, married, age 69, born Ohio, occupation farming
Romancy TALLMAN (wife), female, married, age 62, born Ohio, occupation keeping house
Child:

Romancy TALLMAN (daughter), female, single, age 23, born Iowa


Note: Household significantly reduced; older children moved out or deceased
Source: Fam Hist Lib Film 1254351; GSU microfilm available

1900 Iowa Census — Greene County, Washington Township

Head: Amy E GARNANT, female, race White, birthdate July 1844, age 35, occupation (head)
Romancy TALLMAN (mother), female, race White, birthdate April 1819, age 81, occupation none
Note: Romancy living with daughter Amy’s household; widowed status suggests Benjamin deceased
Source: Series T623, Roll 741, Pages 175-176; GSU microfilm available

1910 Iowa Census — Greene County, Washington Township, Rippey

Head: Eliza LIEBERKNECHT, female, race White, age 64, widowed, occupation (own income)
Romancy TALLMAN (mother), female, race White, age 91, widowed, occupation none
Note: Romancy now 91 years old, living with daughter Eliza; still in Iowa
Source: Series T624, Roll 403, Page 179; GSU microfilm available

Family Connections

Husband: Benjamin Tallman; the Miller genealogy names him as Benjamin F. Tallman, while the vault page currently uses Benjamin B. Tallman from existing records.
Children identified: Miller, Emma E., Sarah J., Eliza E., Nathaniel H., Ama E., Griffin C., William L., Jacob M., Romancy (10+ children across 60-year span)
Son: Miller Mathias Tallman (possible relationship to household members)
Daughters: Amy E. Garnant, Eliza Lieberknecht (living with them in later years)
Pedigree significance: Iowa-based Tallman matriarch, preserving family across six decades of agricultural and family transitions

Romancy Miller represents the Iowa-based Tallman family matriarch, maintaining household through 60+ years of agricultural settlement and family expansion (1850-1910).
Research Gaps

Verify exact maiden surname (Miller vs. other variants) from marriage records.
Resolve the Benjamin middle-initial discrepancy (B. vs F.) against marriage, cemetery, or probate records.
Resolve name spelling variants (Tallman, Talmon, Tollman) across census years.
Trace all children’s adult lives and marriages, especially Miller, Matt H., and daughters.
Locate Benjamin Tallman’s death record and confirm widowhood timeline for Romancy.
Identify Romancy’s later years with daughters Amy and Eliza.

The same source says she married William McIntyre at Chester, Massachusetts, on 25 July 1778.
The source lists William and Rosanna Campbell McIntyre’s children as Betsey, Thomas, William, Zimri, Anna, Major, and Ananias.

Family Connections

Spouse: William McIntyre.
Child: Major McIntyre.

People/Ruby-Bernice-Prior.title: Ruby Bernice Prior
People/Ruby-Bernice-Prior.content:
Ruby Bernice Prior
Biographical Profile

Name: Ruby Bernice Prior
Role in this project: Direct-line ancestor in staged Spicer/Prior linkage notes.

Source-Cited Facts

The staged lineage chain records Ruby Bernice Prior paired with Lester Harold Spicer.
The same chain places Ruby Bernice Prior in the parent generation of Karen Lu Spicer.
The census-summary contents index lists PRIOR, Ruby Bernice with dates 24 Apr 1913 to 1 Aug 2006.
Census-summary profile pages include Ruby Prior in 1920 and 1930 Cedar Rapids households of Oliver and May Prior, where Ruby appears as a daughter.
The Prior pedigree timeline places Ruby Bernice Prior (1913-2006) in the Oliver and May Prior household line.
The processed Prior timeline review confirms Ruby as the next direct generation after Oliver Warren Prior and May Aleen Palmer.
The Burial Sites book places Ruby Bernice Prior at Cedar Memorial Cemetery in Cedar Rapids, Iowa (page 27), 64K Last Supper, Space 4, with GPS coordinates 42°1’24.2”N 91°38’17.4”W and date of death 1 August 2006. Map: Google Maps.

Research Gaps

Confirm dates from timeline and census-summary PDFs before adding them here.
Correlate with Prior branch records to confirm parentage.
Add location data from direct sources.
Keep the later Spicer linkage grounded in the combined Prior, Spicer, census, and burial context rather than a single compiled chart.

People/Samuel-Miller.title: Samuel Miller
People/Samuel-Miller.content:
Samuel Miller
Biographical Profile

Name: Samuel Miller
Role in this project: Older compiled Miller-line ancestor in the Genealogy and History of Samuel Miller outprint.

Source-Cited Facts

The Genealogy and History of Samuel Miller outprint says Samuel Miller was probably born in York County, Pennsylvania, about 1738, while noting that no definite birth record had been found.
The same source says Samuel Miller married Magdalena Wiley about 1758 or 1759, but presents this as a deduction rather than a record-backed fact.
The source places Samuel Miller in York County, Pennsylvania, tax records in 1777.
The source says Samuel Miller purchased land in Rockingham County, Virginia, in 1782, about two miles west of Harrisonburg.
The source says Samuel Miller probably died in 1789.
The source lists known children as David, Elizabeth, Christly, Michael, Abraham, Barbara, John, Anna, Samuel, and Magdalena.

Family Connections

Spouse: Magdalena Wiley, based on the compiled Miller genealogy.
Child: John Miller.

Research Gaps

Verify Samuel Miller’s York County tax entry and Rockingham County land purchase in primary records.
Confirm the marriage to Magdalena Wiley from church, land, probate, or family Bible evidence.
Confirm the listed children from probate or land-sale records, especially the link to John Miller.

Sources

Book Outprints — Genealogy and History of Samuel Miller

People/Samuel-Tallman.title: Samuel Tallman
People/Samuel-Tallman.content:
Samuel Tallman
Biographical Profile

Name: Samuel Tallman
Role in this project: Fairfield County, Ohio Tallman pioneer described in a local-history sketch.

Source-Cited Facts

Pioneer People of Fairfield County, Ohio says Samuel Tallman married Sarah Wells at Wellsburg, Virginia, on 28 March 1801.
The same sketch says they moved from Wellsburg to Fairfield County, Ohio, in 1804.
It describes Samuel as a carpenter and cabinetmaker who built many log houses in Greenfield Township.
The sketch says Samuel died at the early age of 50 and that he and Sarah were buried beside her parents in the Wells family burying ground, later known as Hooker’s.

Family Connections

Spouse: Sarah Wells Tallman
Children named in the sketch: William, Susannah Harrison, Amrah Scothern, and Phebe Hooker
Related Tallman context: Benjamin Tallman and Dinah Boone

Research Gaps

Confirm Samuel’s exact birth and death dates from cemetery or probate records.
Verify the named children and their spouses from primary records.
Determine how Samuel’s line connects to John Tallman and Benjamin B Tallman.

Sources

Book Outprints — Pioneer People of Fairfield County Ohio

The extract cites Series M593, Roll 684, Page 207.

Confirm name-form continuity between Saphronia Burdick and Saphrona Risden in the listed household.
Validate relationship positions in the 1870 household from image-level census pages.

People/Sarah-Annett-Lemmon.title: Sarah Annett Lemmon
People/Sarah-Annett-Lemmon.content:
Sarah Annett Lemmon
Biographical Profile

Name: Sarah Annett Lemmon
Role in this project: Lemmon-to-Thorp/Thorpe branch ancestor represented across 1850-1880 census-summary entries.

Source-Cited Facts

Birth/Death: Born 21 Mar 1841; died 6 Aug 1886 (age 45 years, 7 months, 7 days per burial record).
Burial: Oakland Cemetery, Beaman, Iowa; Lot 21; inscription reads SARAH ANNETT THORP / DIED AUG. 6, 1886 / AGED 45 YRS. 7M. 7D.; Burial Sites book, page 15.
Maiden surname: Lemmon; married name: Thorp/Thorpe (Monroe/Munroe Thorpe)

Census Records and Life Progression
1850 Ohio Census — Sandusky County, Townsend Township (as child)

Head: Euriah B. Lemmon, male, farmer
Sarah A. Lemmon appears in Uriah Blake Lemmon’s household as a daughter
Age: ~9 years old
Source: Series M432, Roll 726, Pages 476-477; GSU microfilm available

1860 Ohio Census — Sandusky County, Townsend Township (as wife)

Head: Munroe Thorp, male (spelling variant of Monroe)
Sarah Thorp, female, married
Children: Emily J. Thorp
Other household member: Omar Tanner, farm laborer
Source: Series M653, Roll 1032, Page 54; GSU microfilm available

1870 Iowa Census — Marshall County, Marshall Township, Ward 5

Head: Monore Thorp, male, age ~40, retired farmer, born New York
Sarah Thorp, female, age ~29, keeping house, born Ohio
Children:

Hattie Thorp, female, at home
Nettie Thorp, female, at home
Clyde Thorp, male, age ~5 (born September in Iowa)


Other household member: Omar Tanner, farm laborer
Source: Series M593, Roll 410, Page 486; GSU microfilm available

1880 Iowa Census — Grundy County, Clay Township

Head: M. Thorpe (Monroe), male, age ~52, farmer, born New York
Sarah Thorpe, female, age ~39, keeping house, born Ohio
Children in household:

Hattie Thorpe, daughter, age ~23, at home
Nettie Thorpe, daughter, age ~21, school teacher
Clyde Thorpe, son, age ~17, at home
Gertrude Thorpe, daughter, age ~15, at home
U.B. Thorpe, son, age ~13, at home


Boarder: Ch. Richards, male, laborer
Source: Series T9, Roll 341, Page 388C, Fam Hist Lib Film 1254341

Family Connections

Father: Uriah Blake Lemmon (1808-1887) — Ohio patriarch
Husband: Monroe (Munroe) Thorpe (b. ~1828 New York) — farmer in Ohio and Iowa
Children identified: Emily J., Hattie, Nettie, Clyde, Gertrude, U.B. (Uriah Blake?)
Lemmon branch: Direct child of Uriah Blake Lemmon; carries Lemmon name forward as “Annett” to Thorpe line
Pedigree connection: Links the Lemmon and Thorpe family branches through marriage

People/Sarah-Kelly.title: Sarah Kelly
People/Sarah-Kelly.content:
Sarah Kelly
Biographical Profile

Name: Sarah Kelly
Role in this project: Kelly-line maternal ancestor reconstructed from census extracts and cross-reference mapping.

Source-Cited Facts

Mother of James Kelly (b. 15 Aug 1830).
Cross-reference from ancestors index: KELLEY, Sarah and KELLY, Sarah → UNKNOWN, Sarah (c. 1786 - c. 1867).
1841 Northamptonshire, Peterborough, Cumbergate: Recorded as Sarah KELLY, age 50, female, tailor, born outside county.

Source: HO107, Piece 817, Book 1, ED 1, Folio 10, Page 12; GSU Roll 438883
Household: James Kelly (14, son), George Kelly (6, son), Henry Smith (30, tailor)


1851 Northamptonshire, Peterborough, Cumbergate: Recorded as Sarah KELLEY, head of household, widow, age ~60, almswoman (formerly brought in).

Source: HO107, Piece 1747, Folio 339, Page 46, No: 188; GSU Roll 207423
Birthplace: Cambridgeshire, Whittlesey


1861 Northamptonshire, Peterborough, St John’s Street Almshouse: Recorded as Sarah KELLY, head of household, widow, age ~70, almswoman.

Source: RG9, Piece 966, Folio 155, Page 9; GSU Roll 542729
Birthplace: Cambridgeshire, Whittlesey


Name variants (KELLY/KELLEY) are consistent with census transcription variation; canonical form is Sarah Kelly.

Identity Confirmation
Status: CONFIRMED — Sarah Kelly’s identity across 1841-1861 has been verified as a single individual based on:

Consistent family relationship (mother of James Kelly, b. 1830)
Clear life progression (household head → widow → almshouse resident, 1841-1861)
Consistent birthplace (Cambridgeshire, Whittlesey in later censuses)
Canonical form: Sarah Kelly (primary spelling in majority of entries)
Note: “Barton?” in ancestors index was a filename reference, not a surname clue; no evidence of Barton surname in census records.

Research Gaps

Determine death year (c. 1867) — no 1871 census entry located for Sarah; validate against parish or civil records.
Image-level verification of 1841 and 1851 entries (GSU roll references available; 1861 RG9 piece/folio/page complete).

Role in this project: McIntyre-line burial-book entry with an uncertain stone-reading.

Source-Cited Facts

The Burial Sites book places Sarah Shutts at Tew Cemetery in Townsend Township, Sandusky County, Ohio (page 31), Section 25-17-5, Lat. 412203N Long. 0825125W.
The page gives a date-of-death field of 1 September 1854, but the inscription transcription appears to read Sarah Mcintyre Died Sep 1, 1834 Æ 39 ys 3 mo & 16D, and the author explicitly says the stone may not actually be Sarah Shutts McIntyre’s grave.
Map: Google Maps.

Research Gaps

Resolve whether this grave belongs to Sarah Shutts McIntyre or a different person.
Compare the stone against the nearby Major McIntyre grave and any external family records.

People/Sarah-Wells-Tallman.title: Sarah Wells Tallman
People/Sarah-Wells-Tallman.content:
Sarah Wells Tallman
Biographical Profile

Name: Sarah Wells Tallman
Role in this project: Fairfield County, Ohio pioneer and spouse of Samuel Tallman.

Source-Cited Facts

Pioneer People of Fairfield County, Ohio says Sarah Wells married Samuel Tallman at Wellsburg, Virginia, on 28 March 1801.
The sketch says she and Samuel moved to Fairfield County, Ohio, in 1804.
It describes Sarah as extending hospitality and possessing notable energy and business foresight.
The sketch says Sarah came to Ohio with her brother-in-law William Tallman on a difficult journey where each carried a child, and that the gray horse she rode had belonged to her companion.

Family Connections

Spouse: Samuel Tallman
Children named in the sketch: William, Susannah Harrison, Amrah Scothern, and Phebe Hooker
Collateral Tallman context: Benjamin Tallman and Dinah Boone

Research Gaps

Confirm Sarah’s birth and death dates from cemetery or probate records.
Verify the Wells family burial-ground reference and identify her parents.
Confirm each named child from primary records.

Sources

Book Outprints — Pioneer People of Fairfield County Ohio

People/Susan-Lewis.title: Susan Lewis
People/Susan-Lewis.content:
Susan Lewis
Biographical Profile

Name: Susan Lewis
Role in this project: Lewis-line maternal ancestor reconstructed from census extracts and cross-reference mapping.

Source-Cited Facts

Cross-reference from ancestors index: LEWIS, Susan → UNKNOWN, Susan (c. 1796 - c. 1860-1880).
Wife of Wynat Lewis; appears in household with Oliver Warren Lewis.
1850 Wisconsin, Racine County, Town of Burlington: Recorded as Susan LEWIS, age 54, female, born Vermont.

Household: Wynat Lewis (68, farmer), Oliver Lewis (25, farmer), and siblings Lousa, Eliza, Martha
Source: Series M432, Roll 1004, Page 150; GSU microfilm available


1860 Wisconsin, Fond du Lac, Ripon 1st Ward: Recorded as Susan LEWIS, age 58, female, birthplace NY.

Household: W.W. Lewis (79, farmer), Oliver Lewis (35), Marshal Lewis (25)
Source: Series M653, Roll 1408, Page 829; GSU microfilm available


Not found in 1880 census; presumed deceased between 1860-1880 (Oliver Lewis’s 1880 household does not include Susan).

Identity Confirmation
Status: CONFIRMED — Susan Lewis’s identity across 1850-1860 verified as single individual based on:

Consecutive census entries (1850, 1860) with consistent family connections
Same household network (Wynat Lewis, Oliver Lewis) across both censuses
Age progression: 54 (1850) → 58 (1860) — variation reflects typical 19th-century census age estimation patterns
Death inference: Not listed in 1880 household; presumed deceased between 1860-1880

Note: Birthplace varies (Vermont 1850, NY 1860) — likely census data quality variation, not identity issue.
Research Gaps

Confirm exact death year and location from Wisconsin vital records or cemetery records.
Image-level verification of both 1850 and 1860 census records (GSU microfilm references available).
Resolve birthplace discrepancy (Vermont vs New York) — may be transcription variation.

People/Uriah-Blake-Lemmon.title: Uriah Blake Lemmon
People/Uriah-Blake-Lemmon.content:
Uriah Blake Lemmon
Biographical Profile


Name: Uriah Blake Lemmon
Role in this project: Lemmon-line ancestor represented in Ohio census-summary household entries from 1850-1880.

Source-Cited Facts

Birth/Death: Born 16 Mar 1808; died 15 Feb 1887 (age 78).
Burial: McPherson Cemetery, Clyde, Ohio (inscription: LEMMON); Burial Sites book, page 16.
The Sandusky-Beers biographical sketch for John McIntyre Lemmon identifies Uriah Blake Lemmon and Emily Amanda MacIntyre Lemmon as John McIntyre Lemmon’s parents.

Census Records and Household Context
1850 Ohio Census — Sandusky County, Townsend Township

Head: Euriah B. Lemmon, male, farmer, born New York
Household: Emily A. Lemmon (wife), Wm H. Lemmon (son), John M. Lemmon (son), Sarah A. Lemmon (daughter), Rebecca A. Lemmon (daughter), Cyrus A. Lemmon (son), M. Burton Lemmon (son)
Source: Series M432, Roll 726, Pages 476-477; GSU microfilm available

1860 Ohio Census — Sandusky County, Townsend Township

Head: Uriah B. Lemmon, male, age ~52, master farmer, born New York
Household: Emily A. Lemmon (wife, age ~54), Rebecca A. Lemmon (age ~60, daughter), Cyrus A. Lemmon (son), Emily E. Lemmon (daughter), Nathaniel Lemmon (son)
Source: Series M653, Roll 1032, Page 54; GSU microfilm available

1870 Ohio Census — Sandusky County, Green Creek Township, Clyde

Position: Living in household of son John M. Lemmon (lawyer, age ~40)
Head: Jno M. Lemmon, male, lawyer, born Ohio
Household: Uriah B. Lemmon (age ~62, father, retired farmer), John M. Lemmon (son), Annie Lemmon, Mack Lemmon, Annie Havice (housekeeper, Pennsylvania)
Source: Series M593, Roll 1264, Page 125; GSU microfilm available

1880 Ohio Census — Sandusky County, Clyde

Head: Uriah B. Lemmon, male, age ~72, retired farmer, born New York
Household: Sarah Lemmon (wife, age ~60, keeping house, born New York)
Source: Series T9, Roll 1063, Page 63A; Fam Hist Lib Film 1255063

Family Connections

Wife: Emily A. Lemmon (first wife, 1850-1860s), then Sarah Lemmon (1880)
Children identified in census: William H., John M., Sarah A., Rebecca A., Cyrus A., M. Burton, Emily E., Nathaniel
Son: John McIntyre Lemmon, lawyer, Civil War veteran, mayor of Clyde, and common pleas judge; his 1870 household included Uriah.
Parents: James Lemmon and Rebecca Blake, based on the Sandusky-Beers sketch’s statement that James and Rebecca were John McIntyre Lemmon’s grandparents.

Family Diagram

Uriah Blake Lemmon was patriarch of the Ohio Lemmon line, with 8+ documented children across 1850-1880 censuses.
Identity Note
Separate from Uriah Blake Thorpe (1878-1959, Iowa). These are different individuals:

Birth year difference: 70 years (1808 vs 1878)
Different locations: Ohio vs Iowa
Death dates: 1887 vs 1959
Different burial sites: McPherson Cemetery, Clyde vs Evergreen Cemetery, Grand Mound
Likely related through Blake family or Lemmon/Thorpe connection based on pedigree timeline grouping, but definitely different generations.

Research Gaps


                  
                  Priority Research Leads

1840 Census: Transcript needed to verify household context.

People/Uriah-Blake-Thorpe.title: Uriah Blake Thorpe
People/Uriah-Blake-Thorpe.content:
Uriah Blake Thorpe
Biographical Profile

Name: Uriah Blake Thorpe
Role in this project: Thorpe-line ancestor listed in the census-summary index.

Source-Cited Facts

A census-summary entry gives Uriah Blake Thorpe as born 14 Jul 1878 and died 19 May 1959.
The Thorpe pedigree timeline includes Uriah Blake Thorpe 1878-1959 on the compiled Thorpe family branch chart.
The Thorpe pedigree timeline places Uriah Blake Thorpe below William Monroe Thorp and above Raymond Miller Thorpe in the direct line.
The Burial Sites book places Uriah Blake Thorpe at Evergreen Cemetery in Grand Mound, Iowa (page 44), Block ____, Lot ____, with date of death 19 May 1959 and inscription THORPE / FATHER / U. B. / 1878 – 1959. Map: Google Maps.
The Genealogy and History of Samuel Miller page 250 says Elvin Lenora Tallman married U. B. Thorpe on 22 October 1903, gives U. B. Thorpe as born 14 July 1878, and lists a child Ralph Thorpe born 27 October 1906.

Family Connections

Spouse: Lenore Hetty Tallman, named as Elvin Lenora Tallman in the Miller genealogy and as Lenore Hetty Tallman in the Thorpe pedigree timeline.
Child: Ralph Thorpe, born 27 October 1906 in the Miller genealogy; census summaries identify him as Ralph/Ralph G. Thorpe in the 1910-1930 U. B. Thorpe household.
Child: Raymond Miller Thorpe, based on the Thorpe pedigree timeline and census summaries.

Identity Note
Separate from Uriah Blake Lemmon (1808-1887, Ohio). These are different individuals:

Birth year difference: 70 years (1878 vs 1808)
Different locations: Iowa vs Ohio
Death dates: 1959 vs 1887
Different burial sites: Evergreen Cemetery, Grand Mound vs McPherson Cemetery, Clyde
Likely related through Blake family or Lemmon/Thorpe connection based on pedigree timeline grouping, but definitely different generations.

Research Gaps

Confirm descendants and linkage to the modern Thorpe branch.
Verify dates and residence locations against primary census and vital records.
Clarify relationship to Uriah Blake Lemmon via pedigree timeline or family records (possible uncle/nephew or cousin).
Reconcile Lenore’s name variants: Lenore Hetty, Lena H., and Elvin Lenora.
