Topics/Spicer-Risden-Branch-Summary.title: Spicer Risden Branch Summary
Topics/Spicer-Risden-Branch-Summary.content:
Spicer and Risden Branch Summary
The Spicer pedigree timeline cleanup tightened this branch in one important way: the later Charles-to-George-to-Lester line is solid branch navigation, while the earlier repeated-Nathan Spicer segment still needs to remain openly unresolved.
Branch Diagram

graph TD
  NS1["Nathan Spicer<br/>c1756-?"]
  NS2["Nathan Spicer<br/>c1756-1845"]
  NS3["Nathan Spicer<br/>c1796-1873"]
  CT["Claramon Tiffany<br/>1799-1835<br/>unresolved placement"]
  CRS["Charles Russell Spicer<br/>1822-1887"]
  GBS["George B. Spicer<br/>1864-1938"]
  HMR["Hattie May Risden<br/>1877-1967"]
  LHS["Lester Harold Spicer<br/>1906-1974"]
  OMR["Onesimus Risden<br/>1760-1848"]
  JWR["John Wheeler Risden<br/>c1816-1884 on chart"]
  WMR["Watson Moses Risden<br/>1843-1932"]
  MB["Mary Burgett<br/>1835-1918"]

  NS1 --> NS2
  NS2 --> NS3
  NS3 -. repeated Nathan zone .-> CRS
  CT -. unresolved chart placement .- CRS
  CRS --- MB
  CRS --> GBS
  GBS --- HMR
  GBS --> LHS
  OMR --> JWR
  JWR --> WMR
  WMR --> HMR

What We Know

The later line from Charles Russell Spicer to George B. Spicer to Lester Harold Spicer is clear on the chart.
Mary Burgett is clearly the Burgett-side spouse attached to the Charles Russell Spicer generation.
The Risden collateral line clearly reaches Watson Moses Risden and Hattie May Risden.

What Remains Uncertain

The repeated-Nathan Spicer sequence between the 1796-1873 Nathan and Charles Russell Spicer remains unresolved.
Claramon Tiffany is not a safe resolved spouse placement from the chart alone.
John Wheeler Risden remains a chart-versus-vault date conflict case.

Topics/Thorpe-Pedigree-Timelines.title: Thorpe Pedigree Timelines
Topics/Thorpe-Pedigree-Timelines.content:
Thorpe Pedigree Timelines
This page tracks the pedigree timeline files provided by Robert “Butch” Thorpe and the processed indexes built from their PDF exports.
Files Provided

References/raw/PedigreeTimelines2025.cdr
References/raw/PedigreeTimelines2019Descendants2.cdr

Processed Timeline Indexes

Thorpe Pedigree Timeline Extraction Index
Bellamy Pedigree Timeline Extraction Index
Prior Pedigree Timeline Extraction Index
Spicer Pedigree Timeline Extraction Index

Public Intake Notes

Thorpe
Bellamy
Prior
Spicer

Butch’s Notes on How to Read the Timelines
Per Butch’s email:

Horizontal bars represent ancestor lifespans.
Red dots indicate official records.
Numbers by red dots refer to Butch’s personal record copies.
Blue dots indicate census records corresponding to the date marked by vertical lines.
The charts show selected early generations that fit on each page, not all ancestors with records.

Current Working Status

SVG Extraction Complete: The high-resolution .xhtml exports have been programmatically parsed (2026-04-30).
Automated Enrichement: Individual lifespan snippets and source indicators (Census, Certificates, Obituaries) have been injected into all relevant person pages.
Reference Numbers: Butch’s personal record reference numbers (e.g., #052, #250) have been extracted and linked to individuals.

Master Pedigree Overview
The following diagram summarizes the convergence of the four main branches documented in the pedigree timelines.
graph TD
  subgraph "Thorpe / Lemmon Branch"
    JT["John Thorp<br/>1791-1850"] --> WMT["William Monroe Thorp<br/>1835-1899"]
    WMT --> UBT["Uriah Blake Thorpe<br/>1878-1959"]
    UBT --> RMT["Raymond Miller Thorpe<br/>1917-1974"]
  end

  subgraph "Bellamy / Thorogood Branch"
    JB["James Bellamy<br/>c1787-1867"] --> RB["Richard Bellamy<br/>c1816-1902"]
    RB --> HJB["Henry James Bellamy<br/>1862-1948"]
    HJB --> JAB["James Archibald Bellamy<br/>1888-1982"]
  end

  subgraph "Prior / Lewis Branch"
    JWP["Joseph Warren Prior<br/>1828-1909"] --> AEP["Arthur Edwin Prior<br/>1853-1929"]
    AEP --> OWP["Oliver Warren Prior<br/>1880-1949"]
    OWP --> RBP["Ruby Bernice Prior<br/>1913-2006"]
  end

  subgraph "Spicer / Risden Branch"
    CRS["Charles Russell Spicer<br/>1822-1887"] --> GBS["George B Spicer<br/>1864-1938"]
    GBS --> LHS["Lester Harold Spicer<br/>1906-1974"]
  end

  RMT --- GBS
  JAB --- RBP

Record-Reference Leads
Thorpe / Lemmon / Tallman / Ault

The Thorpe chart clearly carries John Thorp 1791-1850 → William Monroe Thorp 1835-1899 → Uriah Blake Thorpe 1878-1959 → Raymond Miller Thorpe 1917-1974.
It also visibly places Jane Wager/Jennie Dodge, Sarah Annett Lemmon, and Lenore Hetty Tallman in the same direct-line context.

Bellamy / Kelly / Emblow / Munson / Thorogood / Sorrell

The Bellamy chart clearly carries the later line James Bellamy c1787-1867 → Richard Bellamy c1816-1902 → Henry James Bellamy 1862-1948 → James Archibald Bellamy 1888-1982.
The earliest Bellamy overlap above James remains unresolved.
The same chart provides useful collateral placement for Kelly, Emblow, Munson, Thorogood, and Sorrell pages already in the vault.

Prior / Lewis / Palmer / Bangle

The Prior chart clearly carries Joseph Warren Washington Prior 1828-1909 → Arthur Edwin Prior 1853-1929 → Oliver Warren Prior 1880-1949 → Ruby Bernice Prior 1913-2006.
It also ties Martha Eliza Lewis into the direct Prior chain and shows May Aleen Palmer as the later spouse line.
Oliver Elhanon Lewis, Elizabeth Quackenbush, and the Bangle line remain compiled-chart collateral rather than fully reconciled proof.

Spicer / Risden / Burgett

The Spicer chart clearly supports the later chain Charles Russell Spicer 1822-1887 → George B Spicer 1864-1938 → Lester Harold Spicer 1906-1974.
The earlier repeated-Nathan Spicer segment and Claramon Tiffany placement remain unresolved and should stay marked that way.
The same chart provides a clear Risden side line from Onesimus Risden to Hattie May Risden.

Main Discrepancy Candidates









































PersonTimeline readingOther vault evidenceCurrent handlingArthur Edwin Prior1853-1929Some local census-summary treatment uses 1851-1929Keep birth year open.Elizabeth A Quackenbushc1841-1909Existing page uses c1836-1909Keep as unresolved discrepancy.John Wheeler Risdenc1816-1884Existing local notes use different datesKeep as unresolved discrepancy.James Kellyc1828-before 1881? with Sarah Barton?Parish and census material point elsewhereTreat chart as compiled lead, not resolution.Claramon Tiffany placementRepeated-Nathan zone in the Spicer chartExisting pages previously flattened this into a spouse claimKeep unresolved.
Record-Reference Leads

The processed indexes preserve chart-only marker systems such as numbered record references, CD tags, burial markers, obituary markers, and alphanumeric IDs.
These remain research leads only until tied back to stronger local source material.

Topics/UK-Parish-and-Regional-Context.title: UK Parish and Regional Context
Topics/UK-Parish-and-Regional-Context.content:
UK Parish and Regional Context
Overview
The Thorpe family research vault captures ancestors documented in UK parishes during the 1841–1871 census era, primarily concentrated in the East Midlands and East Anglia regions. This page provides geographic context for understanding settlement patterns and census documentation.
Primary Parishes and Regions
Lincolnshire (East Midlands)
Key Families: Bellamy, Kelly, Emblow, Sorrell, Thorogood

Bourn — Center of Bellamy family documentation (1841–1871)

James Bellamy (1787–c.1867) — Miller, later Bedehouse Man
Richard Bellamy — Agricultural laborer
Henry James Bellamy — Related line


Lincolnshire counties documentation: HO107 (1841), RG9 (1861), RG10 (1871) series
Migration pattern: Rural Lincolnshire to urban Bedhouses in later life (economic transition)

Related Regions (Implied from Census References)
Somerset/Devon — Sorrell and Thorogood surname distribution suggests West Country origin

Agricultural communities in 1841–1871 census era
Family names scattered across parishes but not yet reconciled to specific locations

County Tyrone, Ireland / Massachusetts — McIntyre compiled-genealogy origin trail

William McIntyre (1754–after 1789) documented in the MacIntyre book outprint as father of Major McIntyre
The same outprint traces an older compiled line to John McIntyre of Kilmoore, County Tyrone, Ireland, in Massachusetts before 1719
US settlement continued into Ohio and Wisconsin in later generations

Census Series and Documentation



































Census YearSeriesCoverageKey Families1841HO107English parishesBellamy, Kelly, Sorrell, Thorogood1851HO107English parishesBellamy (Bedhouses transition)1861RG9English parishesBellamy, related lines1871RG10English parishesLimited; many families in US by this time
Research Implications
Known Gaps

Pre-1841 UK sources (parish registers, Poor Law records) not yet incorporated
Specific street addresses and township boundaries in Lincolnshire not yet mapped
Cemetery/burial locations in UK parishes not yet systematized

Next Steps for Parish Research

Locate Bourn parish registers (baptisms, marriages, burials) for Bellamy line 1787–1840
Map Lincolnshire agricultural communities to identify other family branches
Research Poor Law settlement records for urban transitions (Bedhouse entries)
Verify Somerset/Devon connection for Sorrell/Thorogood lines via parish records

Topics/Visual-Legend.title: Visual Legend for Pedigree Timelines
Topics/Visual-Legend.content:
Visual Legend for Pedigree Timelines
This project utilizes a sophisticated notation system developed by Robert “Butch” Thorpe in his original CorelDRAW pedigree diagrams. These symbols provide high-signal indicators of available records and research status.
Where to Find These Visuals
Every major ancestor in the vault now features a Lifespan Snippet at the very top of their page. This visual bar is extracted directly from the high-resolution pedigree charts and provides an at-a-glance view of their records.
Featured Examples

Ralph Gerald Thorpe: A complete record set including census hits, multiple primary records (red dots), and burial/obituary markers.
Andrew Ault: Shows early 19th-century census verification.
Elizabeth Plomey Ault: A clear view of late-life record availability and burial status.
Lester Harold Spicer: Shows 20th-century timeline placement with associated records.

Symbols and Indicators
Lifespan Bars


Horizontal Bar: Represents the verified or estimated lifespan of an ancestor.
Background Grid: Vertical lines represent 50-year increments (1700, 1750, 1800, etc.).

Record Indicators (Dots)

● Solid Red Circle: Indicates a Primary Record or Certificate (Birth, Death, or Marriage) is held in the Thorpe family archive.
Reference Numbers: Solid red dots are often accompanied by 3-digit numbers (e.g., #052, #250). These are found in the Source Indicators section of the person page and correspond to Butch’s physical filing system.
○ Hollow Red Circle: Indicates a possible certificate or record that has been identified but not yet obtained.
● Solid Blue Circle: Indicates the individual was Enumerated in the Census for that year. The year is calculated based on the dot’s horizontal position.
○ Hollow Blue Circle: Indicates a possible census match requiring further verification.

Status Icons

RIP Marker: The “RIP” icon indicates the Burial Site has been determined, often through physical visitation or cemetery record verification.
Obit Marker: The “Obit” icon indicates a copy of the individual’s Obituary is available in the archive.

Automated Extraction Note
As of April 30, 2026, these indicators have been programmatically extracted from the original SVG sources. This data is now searchable and cross-referenced in the  Source Indicators section of each person’s profile, located just above the Sources list.

index.title: Thorpe Family History
index.content:
Thorpe Family History

This site gathers the Thorpe family history shared by Robert “Butch” Thorpe and turns it into a source-grounded family wiki. It follows the Thorpe line through connected Lemmon, Blake, Spicer, Risden, Palmer, Prior, Lewis, Ault, and Tallman families.
The best way to begin is with one of the branch stories below. Each branch page gives a plain-language overview, a compact family diagram, links to the strongest person profiles, and a short list of what still needs verification.
Start With a Family Branch

























BranchWhat to expectLemmon, Blake, and ThorpeThe core Thorpe-connected branch context from the compiled pedigree timeline, including the two separate Uriah Blake identities and the Sarah Annett Lemmon bridge into the Thorpe line.Spicer and RisdenAn Iowa farming line from Nathan Spicer through George B. Spicer, Hattie May Risden, and Lester Harold Spicer.Palmer, Prior, and LewisA migration story from Wisconsin and Minnesota into Iowa, connecting Wynant Williamson Lewis, Martha Eliza Lewis, May Aleen Palmer, and the Prior family.Ault and TallmanOhio-to-Iowa household records for the Ault and Tallman families, including Elizabeth Plomey Ault, Miller Mathias Tallman, Benjamin B Tallman, and Romancy Miller.
Explore by Need

Find a person: Use the People Directory for the full alphabetical list.
Read the family stories: Explore Family Stories and Biographies for narrative accounts of ancestor lives, including Civil War service.
Understand the visuals: Read the Visual Legend for Pedigree Timelines to understand the color-coded dots and icons used on person profiles.
See who lived when: Visit the Chronological Portal to explore overlapping lifespans and era-based timelines.
Search by topic or source: Use the Search Index.
See the source trail: Start with Butch Thorpe Email, Thorpe Pedigree Timelines, and Census InDesign Summaries.

Site Progress
The current vault documents over 120 people and includes census extracts, burial-site notes, and compiled pedigree timelines. As of April 30, 2026, the site features automated SVG timeline snippets for all individuals extracted from the original CorelDRAW diagrams, providing a visual-first view of their records and lifespans.

Follow recent work: Read the Changelog for completed reconciliations, newly enriched profiles, and publishing notes.

What This Site Is
This is a research wiki, not a finished family book. Some pages are well supported by census summaries, burial records, and pedigree timeline exports; other pages are still placeholders for future verification. When the evidence is uncertain, the page should say so.
The current vault documents 111 people and includes census extracts, burial-site notes, compiled pedigree timelines, and references to 31 genealogical or historical book extracts. The strongest visitor-ready pages are linked from the branch summaries above.
ove.
